Sport Climbing · Lead & Boulder

Nika Potapova

Ukrainian. Based in Paris. Self-managed from the start. One of the strongest lead climbers in the field — and almost no one outside the sport knows her name yet.

Nationality
Ukrainian
Base
Paris, France
Discipline
Lead / Boulder
Season
2025 — Active

The story

Nika Potapova has been managing her own career since she was a teenager. No personal coach. No federation support to speak of. No budget for physio or nutrition advice. Every competition trip — every flight, every floor to sleep on, every borrowed car seat — organised herself, often by calling friends in the right cities and asking if she had somewhere to stay.

She grew up in Ukraine, trained obsessively, and broke records early. At 14, she set an unofficial women's world record on the Slackboard — and came within five seconds of the men's mark. She didn't celebrate it much. She moved on and climbed harder.

"I was just stubborn and fighting till the end. I didn't want to fail, so I was just grabbing all holds so hard to do the top."

— Nika Potapova
Lead climbing, outdoor route

She has competed at Oloron, one of the sport's most prestigious commercial events, making finals and finishing fifth. She has trained out of Innsbruck — one of the best climbing cities in the world — sharing a small apartment with teammates, one of whom slept on the balcony. She has come close to quitting. She hasn't.

Now she's in Paris. The move was deliberate: a bigger city, more doors to knock on, a real attempt to build the commercial side of a career she's been funding herself for years. She has a full competitive calendar and a recovery arc from surgery that she's managing the same way she manages everything else — on her own, patiently, without complaint.

What she's like on the wall

Nika describes competition as the only place where her mind goes quiet. In isolation before finals she puts in her earphones and focuses on her breathing. When she's climbing, she says she stops hearing the crowd entirely.

"When I climb I'm just somewhere in another world and I'm fully in my thoughts. I don't feel that someone is around me. It's just my peaceful moment."

— Nika Potapova

Her best results have come when she stops thinking. She knows this about herself. She also knows she hasn't reached her ceiling yet — and talks about top-top level not as a fantasy but as an unfinished project.

2025 season

Calendar

Context
What this means
Consistent international visibility across six months
European Champs is the season centrepiece — a single, high-profile event with broadcast and press coverage
Paris base creates local brand proximity for European partners

Why this matters for a brand

Sport climbing has been an Olympic discipline since Tokyo 2020. The athlete base is young, engaged, and growing fast — but most of the sport's top competitors are still deeply undercommercialised. Nika sits in a specific gap: experienced enough to perform consistently at the highest level, unknown enough outside climbing that the right brand gets in early.

The opportunity

Nika is Ukrainian, Paris-based, self-made, and competing at European Championship level. She has a story that works beyond the sport — grit, resilience, independence — and a clean slate commercially. She is not expensive. She is not overexposed. And she is not asking brands to take a chance on potential. She is already here.

Red Bull documentary, 2024

She is also, for the right brand, genuinely interesting to follow. The recovery, the move to Paris, the season building — there is a real arc here, and she documents it with the same directness she brings to everything else.
